differential_diagnoses: Viperid envenomation-induced coagulopathy and systemic toxicity
Acute kidney injury (likely secondary to acute tubular necrosis or cortical necrosis)
next_steps: Remove tourniquet immediately and assess neurovascular status
Administer appropriate polyvalent antivenom as indicated for systemic envenomation
Initiate urgent fluid resuscitation and hemodynamic stabilization
Perform immediate blood transfusion (packed red blood cells) given hemoglobin of 7.0 g/dL and active bleeding
Consult obstetrics urgently for evaluation of fetal status and management of vaginal bleeding/abruption
Initiate emergent dialysis for severe azotemia and uremia
Correct coagulopathy with fresh frozen plasma or cryoprecipitate
Monitor vital signs, urine output, and serial coagulation studies continuously
